targetDevice.name: CH32V203C8T6
targetDevice.notes: Exact target variant selected by the user. WCH appears to publish family-level documentation for CH32V20x/30x rather than a C8T6-only datasheet/reference manual.

sources.notes: Official WCH family datasheet for CH32V20x/30x devices. Primary source for device identity, package/pinout, electrical and feature coverage for CH32V203C8T6.
Official WCH family reference manual covering CH32V2x/V3x peripherals and registers. Primary source for register structure, fields, clocks, resets, and peripheral semantics.
Official openwch repository with SDK material, examples, headers, startup code, and package-specific project assets for CH32V20x family devices.
Official WCH peripheral access header. The CH32V20x_D6 grouping explicitly includes CH32V203C8 and is useful for IRQ names, peripheral types, and register definitions.
Official WCH startup file for the D6 subset that includes CH32V203C8. Useful to cross-check exception and interrupt vector ordering.
Official architecture-level processor manual referenced by the openwch datasheet README. Useful for core and exception behavior not fully explained in the device-family manuals.

Community-maintained CH32V203 family SVD from ch32-rs. Not affiliated with WCH, but metadata-rich and useful as an auditable gap-filler for register descriptions, reset values, field descriptions, and some field access semantics when the official manuals and headers are too sparse.

Community-curated exact-variant machine-readable chip description from ch32-data. The project states its data is manually created and curated from official datasheets, MounRiver-distributed SVD files, and openwch material, making it a useful structured cross-check and provenance trail for community-derived metadata.
